name: Philodendron sodiroi 'Variegated'
origin: Colombia; variegated collector form in cultivation
aboutText: Philodendron sodiroi 'Variegated' is a stunning climbing aroid native to Colombia. Prized for its gorgeous, broad ovate-cordate leaves with a shallow sinus, this variety is highly sought after by collectors. In cultivation, it thrives in warm, humid conditions with bright indirect light and vertical support to shingle and climb. Its foliage displays a matte-satin texture adorned with a distinctive silver pattern (mottling) overlaid with beautiful cream-to-pale-yellow marbling or sectoral patches.

morphology.leafShape: Broad ovate-cordate with a shallow sinus
morphology.variegation: Cream to pale-yellow marbling or sectors over silver mottling
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with aerial roots at nodes

propagation.notes: Crawling rhizome species — take cuttings with at least one node from the rhizome. Avoid severing too close to the growing tip.

careGuide.substrate: Chunky, well-draining mix: 40% orchid bark, 30% perlite, 20% sphagnum moss, 10% worm castings. A wide, shallow pot suits the crawling growth habit.
careGuide.watering: Water when the top 3–4 cm is dry. These terrestrial species are slightly more forgiving than climbers but still hate waterlogged roots.
careGuide.humidity: 65–80%. Consistent high humidity produces larger, better-formed leaves.
careGuide.fertilising: Quarter-strength balanced fertiliser every two weeks during the growing season. Ease off in winter.
careGuide.repotting: Repot into a wider (not deeper) container when the rhizome reaches the pot edge. Every 18–24 months typically.

commonProblems.fix: Increase humidity and keep away from windows and vents in winter
Repot into fresh chunky substrate; trim any affected roots
Ensure 18–26°C and a brighter indirect light position

items: Confirm the rhizome is firm throughout — soft spots indicate rot
Check there is at least one healthy growing tip visible
Inspect leaf undersides for thrips (silvery stippling) and scale insects (small brown bumps on petioles)
Crawling philodendrons ship poorly if carelessly packaged — check seller reviews mention careful packing

fieldNotes.title: Sodiroi With the Splash
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A variegated collector form of Philodendron sodiroi — a crawling, rhizomatous species that produces patterned, silver-spotted leaves in the type form. The variegated selection adds cream or white sector variegation on top of the existing silver-grey leaf patterning, creating a plant with layered visual complexity. From Colombia in the wild; variegated form maintained in collector cultivation. At upper middle tier pricing. Sodiroi's crawling growth habit applies to this form as to the type: a wide, shallow pot rather than a moss pole.
